$comment: D-CSUBSET-MUTABLE-CHAR-ARRAY-RODATA (folded into the c67 cycle, 2026-06-30): a MUTABLE file-scope `char m[N]="str"` global is the array OBJECT itself (C 6.7.9 mutable storage) and must live in writable .data, NOT read-only .rodata — a runtime write `m[0]='J'` SEGFAULTs if it is wrongly in .rodata. Root cause: src/asm/asm.cpp lowerMirGlobalsToDataItems' string-literal arm unconditionally forced d.section=Rodata for EVERY std::string-valued global (correct for the SYNTHETIC string-literal-pool global a `char *p="hi"` points to, WRONG for a NAMED user array). Fix: (1) src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp materializeStringLiteralGlobal mints its synthetic string global isConst=true (the bytes are immutable — matching tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr's string arm which already did), so the const-based section selection routes it to .rodata; (2) asm.cpp removes the unconditional .rodata override → the section is chosen by isConst alone: a SYNTHETIC pool global (const) → .rodata, a NAMED mutable `char arr[N]="str"` (non-const declaration) → writable .data. m[0]='J' write succeeds (m in .data); m[1..5] init preserved; const c[] still readable in .rodata; `char *p="hi"` pointed-to bytes still readable in .rodata (regression guard) → 42. PRESERVE: a write THROUGH `char *p="hi"` is UB and may fault — its bytes correctly stay .rodata. RED-ON-DISABLE: restore the unconditional .rodata override (or revert the materializeStringLiteralGlobal isConst) → the `m[0]='J'` write SEGFAULTs (exit 139), not 42.
